mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving earnings revisions upward and lifting equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are flat or negative when UNRATE crosses below 5.0% in more than 60% of out-of-sample instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising new home sales indicates synchronized demand-supply expansion, which historically precedes positive earnings surprises and equity outperformance over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample periods, or partial correlation with SPX returns drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts in a high-interest-rate environment indicate structural demand resilience that supports commercial real estate and construction-related equities, driving short-term positive returns.
failure_condition: HOUST rises while SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered periods, or the interaction term explains less than 3% of return variance.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reduces recession risk premiums and compresses equity credit spreads, producing positive forward returns over a 60-day horizon regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive during consecutive quarters of GDP growth (t-test p > 0.05), or directional accuracy falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment levels exceed 135 million while the unemployment rate remains above 6.0%, it signals a healthy labor market recovery without inflationary pressure, supporting equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are flat or negative when both thresholds are met in more than 60% of instances, or forward returns show no statistical difference from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates supply-side capacity expansion outpacing labor constraints, which historically precedes margin expansion and positive equity returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain more than 5% of forward return variance, or out-of-sample directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary regime, a rising consumer price index signals the end of deflationary fears and supports nominal earnings growth, driving positive short-term equity returns when combined with stable industrial output.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative when CPI rises above the threshold and INDPRO is stable in more than 55% of periods, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward tests.
